signInSheet.subtitleChoose: An account lets you restore your gateway list on a new device. Your chat history and gateway credentials always stay on-device.
signInSheet.subtitleEmail: We'll send a magic link to your inbox — no password needed.
signInSheet.magicLinkSentBody: We sent a sign-in link to {{email}}. Tap the link in your email to complete sign-in.
signInSheet.genericFailedBody: An error occurred. Please try again.
signInSheet.invalidEmailBody: Please enter a valid email address.

pinnedKeys.title: Pinned Keys
pinnedKeys.addPinHint: Paste the SHA-256 SPKI hash from your gateway server. Accepts 64-char hex, base64, or hex with colon separators.
pinnedKeys.noPins: No keys pinned. The app records your gateway's certificate fingerprint on first connection, but won't block mismatches until you add at least one pin above.
pinnedKeys.invalidPinBody: Expected a 64-character hex or 44-character base64 SHA-256 hash.
pinnedKeys.alreadyPinnedBody: This key is already in the pinned set.

pinMismatch.title: Certificate pin mismatch
pinMismatch.body1: The gateway's certificate public key does not match any pin recorded for this server. This could indicate a key rotation on the server side, or a man-in-the-middle attack.
pinMismatch.body2: No data was transmitted. The connection was blocked at the TLS handshake before any application frames were sent.
pinMismatch.verifyTitle: How to verify this on your server
pinMismatch.verifyA11y: How to verify this on your server
pinMismatch.verifyIntro: Run this on the server host to compute the expected SPKI hash and compare it to the value shown above:
pinMismatch.verifyConfirm: The hex output must exactly match the "OBSERVED KEY" shown above before you approve the new key.
pinMismatch.approveBody: Only approve if you initiated a key rotation on your gateway and can independently verify the hash above matches your server's current certificate.
pinMismatch.approvePrompt: Type the last 8 characters of the key ({{suffix}}) to confirm:
pinMismatch.forgetAlertBody: This will remove the server profile, all pinned keys, and the gateway token. You will need to re-add the server and re-pair your device.

pairing.needsApprovalBody: This device needs to be approved on your OpenClaw server before you can chat.
pairing.insecureWarning: Unencrypted connection. Update to wss:// in Settings.

clearMedia.alertBodyEmpty: No cached media on disk. Nothing will be removed.
clearMedia.errorBody: Could not clear media cache. Please try again.

clearChat.subtitle: Removes the encrypted message tail used for cold-start display

clearChat.alertBodyEmpty: No chat cache on disk. Nothing will be removed.
clearChat.errorBody: Could not clear chat cache. Please try again.

experiments.title: Experiments
experiments.description: iOS Voice Control / dictation bisect flags. Restart required — composer TextInput is uncontrolled, so flags apply on remount.
experiments.restartHint: Restart required. Flags apply when the composer remounts.

skipPasteWrapper.label: Skip paste wrapper
skipPasteWrapper.description: Skip expo-paste-input TextInputWrapper. Inline keyboard image paste stops firing onPaste; paperclip still works.

useIntrinsicHeight.label: Use intrinsic height
useIntrinsicHeight.description: Drop hidden mirror Text + setMeasuredHeight path. Sizes TextInput with minHeight/maxHeight only, fewer native layout updates during dictation.

stableProps.label: Stable TextInput props
stableProps.description: Memoize handleTextChange, style array, and textField JSX so prop references are stable across keystrokes. Prevents UITextView from dropping in-flight dictation characters.

logDictation.label: Log dictation ticks
logDictation.description: Capture each onChangeText fired during dictation into an in-memory ring buffer (500 entries). View via the probe row below.

addServer.hintServerName: A friendly name to identify this connection
addServer.hintServerAddress: Hostname, IP, or paste a full URL — the protocol is stripped automatically
addServer.tailnetBody: Make sure Tailscale is running and you're signed in. Use the gateway's actual port (`18789` by default, or `443` if fronted by `tailscale serve`).
addServer.insecureBody: The URL you entered uses an unencrypted scheme (`ws://` / `http://`). It will be **upgraded to `wss://`** automatically, but check that your server supports TLS — your auth token would otherwise be visible on the network.
addServer.tokenHelpIntro: Run on the host where your OpenClaw gateway is installed.
addServer.tokenHelpLegacyLabel: If your config has the token inline:
addServer.forgetDeviceBody: Wipes the device keypair shared by every server profile. You'll need to re-approve this device on every gateway afterwards.
addServer.discardBody: You have unsaved changes. Go back and lose them?
addServer.requiredFieldsError: Please fill in the required fields above
addServer.saveError: Failed to save server profile. Please try again.
addServer.testPassedPairing: Gateway reachable. This device still needs to be approved on the server before it can chat.

errors.certError: TLS certificate error. The server's certificate is not trusted by iOS. Check your gateway's cert, or (if on Tailscale) that it's using a Tailscale-issued cert.
errors.timeout: Connection timed out. The server may not be reachable — check the address and port.
errors.tailnetUnreachable: Can't reach the server. Make sure **Tailscale is connected** on this device and your server is online.
errors.connectionRefused: Connection refused — the server is reachable but nothing is listening on that port. Check that the OpenClaw gateway is running and bound to a reachable interface (not just `localhost`).
errors.hostnameNotFound: Hostname not found — the address couldn't be resolved. Check for **typos** in the server address.
errors.networkGeneric: Can't reach the server. Check the address and your network connection.
errors.authFailed: Authentication failed. The gateway rejected the auth token — make sure it matches the token on the server.
errors.identityStale: This device's identity doesn't match what the server has on file. Tap the trash icon to forget this device, then reconnect.
errors.fallback: Connection failed. Check the URL and token.

conventions.previewCaption: Anything outside the markers is preserved across install / reinstall / uninstall.
conventions.descriptionPrefix: By default, ClawBoy adds a small hidden context primer to
conventions.descriptionSuffix: the first message of each new session in the app to enable Inline Reply Controls. This primer is invisible and free of token cost in other clients.
conventions.previewBubble: Before we proceed, a couple of quick questions:
conventions.previewQ1Prompt: Should we batch the migration or run it row-by-row?
conventions.previewQ2Prompt: When should we run it — weekend or overnight?
conventions.previewQ2ChoiceNightValue: Run it overnight on a weekday
conventions.modeSubtitlePrimer: Per-session primer only (default). Other clients pay no tokens.
conventions.modeSubtitleAuto: Per-session primer + auto-install AGENTS.md on first send. Adds tokens to each turn even for non-ClawBoy clients.
conventions.modeSubtitleOff: Inline Reply Controls disabled. No primer, no AGENTS.md install.
conventions.agentsMdPreviewSubtitle: See the primer text used in the session primer and AGENTS.md

pinnedKeysEdu.certChangeLabel: What is certificate rotation and when does it happen?
pinnedKeysEdu.introBody1: Every TLS certificate has a public key. "Pinning" records the fingerprint of that public key from the TLS certificate on your OpenClaw server, and compares it to the fingerprint of the certificate you're connecting to, every time you connect. If the fingerprints don't match, ClawBoy refuses to connect.
pinnedKeysEdu.introBody2: Without pinning, any certificate authority in your OS trust store can issue a certificate for your gateway hostname, impersonating your server. This can be done by corporate proxies (Zscaler, Netskope) and MDM-installed CAs. Pinning makes that impossible.
pinnedKeysEdu.introBody3: You don't need to enable this to connect to your OpenClaw server. But if you do, and if your server's certificate ever changes legitimately (renewal, key rotation), you'll need to update the pin here before you can reconnect.
pinnedKeysEdu.letsEncryptTitle: Let's Encrypt on a VPS (most common)
pinnedKeysEdu.letsEncryptBody:
Let's Encrypt certificates expire every 90 days. Most server setups (Caddy, nginx + certbot, Traefik) renew them automatically. The domain name stays the same but the key pair is regenerated, so the fingerprint changes.

If ClawBoy blocks your connection after 90 days, this is almost certainly why. Go to Settings → Pinned Keys, tap the new hash in the TOFU record, and pin it.
pinnedKeysEdu.selfSignedBody: If you generated your own cert with openssl, it rotates only when you explicitly run the command again. You control when this happens — just remember to update the pin here after you do.
pinnedKeysEdu.tailscaleBody: Tailscale issues its own certificates for .ts.net hostnames via HTTPS. These are managed by Tailscale and rotate on their schedule. If pinning is active and your Tailscale cert rotates, you'll see a pin mismatch screen. Promote the new TOFU record to a pin to continue.
pinnedKeysEdu.cloudflareBody: The TLS certificate Cloudflare presents to ClawBoy is Cloudflare's own edge certificate, not your origin certificate. Pinning a Cloudflare edge cert is fragile — Cloudflare rotates these without warning. If you use Cloudflare Tunnel, pinning is less useful and may break unexpectedly. Consider running your gateway behind Tailscale instead if you want reliable pinning.
pinnedKeysEdu.openclawBody: If OpenClaw handles certificate management for you (depends on your deployment mode), check your gateway's admin panel or docs for how often certificates rotate and whether rotation generates a new key pair.
pinnedKeysEdu.confirmBody: Once pinned, ClawBoy will refuse to connect if your server's certificate fingerprint ever changes — including on legitimate renewals or key rotations. You'd need to come back here and pin the updated fingerprint to reconnect.
pinnedKeysEdu.verifyBody: The OpenClaw gateway doesn't display this fingerprint in its UI. Run this from any machine that can reach your server, then compare the output to the fingerprint shown below:

user1.content: What can you help me with?

assistant1.thinking: The user wants a tour of capabilities. I should give a concise but impressive overview covering the main use cases without overwhelming them.
assistant1.content:
Welcome to **ClawBoy** — your personal AI gateway client.

I can help you with almost anything:

- **Coding** — write, refactor, debug, and explain code in any language
- **Research** — search the web, summarize documents, analyze data
- **Automation** — control your desktop apps, run scripts, manage files
- **Creative work** — writing, brainstorming, image generation

Type anything below to get started, or browse the sample sessions in the sidebar (☰) to see what's possible.

summary.thinking: The user wants a document summary. I'll demo how I'd condense a document into key points.
summary.reply:
**TL;DR:** Modern AI agents work best when given clear goals, reliable tools, and a feedback loop that lets them course-correct.

**Key points:**

- **Goal clarity** — vague prompts produce vague results; explicit success criteria cut iteration time in half
- **Tool reliability** — agents are only as good as the APIs and data sources they can reach
- **Feedback loops** — streaming output and tool-call visibility let you catch errors early before they compound
- **Context limits** — long documents should be chunked; summaries at each stage prevent the model from losing the thread
- **Human checkpoints** — for high-stakes tasks, a pause-and-confirm step before irreversible actions is worth the latency

*In a live session your gateway can summarize any attached file, paste of text, or URL — just share it and ask.*

brainstorm.thinking: The user wants ideas. I'll fan out a varied, concrete list they can actually act on.
brainstorm.reply:
Here are 5 starter ideas to spark the conversation:

1. **Build a personal knowledge base** — capture notes, bookmarks, and snippets; let the agent surface relevant context on demand
2. **Automate your weekly review** — pull todos, calendar, and inbox into a single structured digest every Friday morning
3. **Create a local dev assistant** — wire your editor to an agent that can run tests, explain errors, and draft PRs
4. **Set up a home-lab monitor** — agent watches logs and metrics, pages you only when something actually matters
5. **Write a living style guide** — describe your writing preferences once; let the agent enforce them across every document

Tell me more about your project and I'll tailor these to your specific situation.

help.thinking: The user wants to understand my capabilities. I'll give a clear, concrete overview.
help.reply:
Happy to help. Here's what I can explain or work through with you:

- **Code** — write, debug, explain, refactor in any language
- **Research** — web search, document analysis, fact-checking
- **Automation** — file management, shell scripts, API calls
- **Creative** — writing, brainstorming, image generation
- **Data** — parse, transform, visualize datasets

This is **demo mode** — responses are scripted. Connect a real OpenClaw gateway in Settings to unlock live AI.

Type anything — I'll do my best!

settings.thinking: The user is asking about connecting to a real server.
settings.reply:
To connect your own OpenClaw gateway:

1. Tap the **⚙️ Settings** icon at the top right
2. Tap **Exit demo & add server**
3. Enter your gateway URL and auth token
4. ClawBoy will pair with your gateway using a secure Ed25519 device key

Your gateway can be running locally (Tailscale), on a VPS, or any machine you control.

fallback.thinking: I'll give a helpful demo response that shows off the thinking-and-reply pattern.
fallback.reply:
That's an interesting question! In demo mode my responses are scripted, so I can't answer every query with live reasoning.

Here are prompts I respond well to in demo mode:
- *"Write a React component"*
- *"Summarize a document"*
- *"Brainstorm ideas for [your topic]"*
- *"Help me understand [a concept]"*

**Bonus:** *"Tell me a joke"* or *"Show me a sunset photo"* also work.

Connect a real OpenClaw gateway in Settings to get fully live AI responses.
